name: Ondansetron
description: Selective 5-HT3 receptor antagonist antiemetic. Highly effective for nausea and vomiting. ODT (orally disintegrating tablet) is useful in field settings when IV unavailable. Included in some combat pill packs.
indications: Nausea and vomiting (chemotherapy, opioid-induced, post-operative, TBI-related)
dose: 4-8 mg IV/IO slow push over 2-5 min. ODT: 4-8 mg dissolved under tongue. IM: 4 mg.
contraindications: Known hypersensitivity to ondansetron or other 5-HT3 antagonists
Congenital long QT syndrome (high-dose IV only)
warnings: High-dose IV may prolong QT interval -- use lowest effective dose IV.
Does not replace airway management in obtunded patient with vomiting -- protect airway first.

pediatric.dose: 0.1-0.15 mg/kg IV (max 4 mg). ODT: 4mg for children > 4 years.
pediatric.note: Widely used and well-tolerated in pediatrics.

obstetric.note: Limited data in pregnancy. Use for severe nausea/vomiting after first trimester when benefits outweigh risks. Not recommended in first trimester.
